description_full:
Founded in 2017, refurbed is Europe’s fastest-growing marketplace for refurbished products, active in 12 European countries and having surpassed €2Bn in GMV — all while being profitable. With beautiful headquarters in Vienna, we operate as a remote-first company, and our 250+ employees enjoy up to two months of workation per year. We’ve also been recognised as a Top DACH Employer by Kununu for three consecutive years.




Our mission is to make sustainable consumption the easy choice by enabling customers to buy products up to 40% cheaper while significantly reducing CO₂ emissions.




If you thrive in an environment that values momentum, ownership, and impact, you’ll feel at home here. We’re a fast-paced, high-performance team that works hard and challenges itself everyday. To enable this high-performance every team member enjoys full autonomy over their location (we’re remote-first).




The Category and Sales Marketing Analyst will play a crucial role in supporting vouchers and promotion operations, multi-category growth, improving supply-demand alignment, and strengthening the commercial foundations needed for successful marketing strategies across multiple categories, markets, and performance channels.




This role will directly enable Refurbed’s expansion across categories, brands and countries by taking ownership of operational and analytical tasks.




You will work closely with Demand Planning, Category Managers, CRM, Performance Marketing (Search/Shopping, Paid Social, Affiliates), Product, and Commercial teams.




WHO YOU ARE
2–5 years experience in e-commerce, category management, marketing operations, performance marketing, or commercial strategy.
Strong analytical and data-handling skills; able to create clear insights from complex data.
Highly organized, reliable, and operationally strong.
Comfortable coordinating with multiple teams and managing parallel workstreams across categories, markets, and performance channels.
Proactive mindset with strong problem-solving skills.
Experience with consumer electronics / SDA is a plus.
Experience with promotions or marketplace operations preferred.
Bonus: performance marketing fundamentals (e.g., Search/Shopping, Paid Social, Affiliates)




Location & eligibility: This role is open to candidates based in Europe (including UK) only with a valid right to work.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

You drive multi-category growth. You support prioritization and budget allocation across categories and markets, turning demand-side insights (search, click data, pricing signals) into clear, actionable growth opportunities and structured recommendations.
You run promotions end-to-end. You execute voucher campaigns across markets, monitor performance and ROI, and continuously optimize based on supply relevance, cost efficiency, and commercial impact.
You connect Marketing and Supply. You align teams by consolidating forecasts, stock, pricing, and partner inputs, ensuring everyone works off the same priorities and timelines while contributing clear insights to business reviews.
You enable brands and partners. You maintain key KPIs, ensure data readiness for activation, and support smooth rollout of brand campaigns.
You scale how we work. You build tools, dashboards, and documentation that enable teams to be more self-serve, while identifying automation opportunities to reduce manual effort.
You support performance execution. You help translate category priorities into performance marketing channels (Search, Paid Social, Affiliates) and ensure campaigns are set up for success.




WHY YOU WILL ENJOY WORKING WITH US:

Our Culture and Values:

Decisions should be made based on facts and not by hierarchy levels? We sure think so!
We embrace giving and receiving feedback, testing and welcoming new ideas, as well as sharing our knowledge across the company.
You fail sometimes? Well, so do we! We’re all just human, let’s learn from our mistakes to improve in the future!
No matter who you love, where you’re from, who you pray to, whether you pee sitting down or standing up – we are a bunch of talented people who enjoy spending our time making a difference!

Personal & Professional Development:

You'll have access to a dedicated learning budget within your department to support your growth, whether it's courses, conferences, or other learning opportunities that matter to you.
We partner with Likeminded, offering you private sessions with experts for your personal growth and mental health.
We frequently host internal workshops, allowing our teams to further develop their skills in different areas and enhancing collaboration across departments.

Flexibility:

We care about a healthy work/life balance. We not only function and support a fully remote setting but also offer the possibility to work from our beautiful office in the vibrant heart of Vienna – we love dogs, so feel free to bring your furry little buddy too 😊
We trust you to do the best job, so we don't care so much when you do it – you are able to self-organize.
You will also be invited to take part in our office weeks, happening three times a year, where you will have the chance to work side by side with your colleagues and enjoy the team-building events and on-site workshops




…and much more! See https://careers.refurbed.com/ for further insights!

description_full:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Category & Marketing Analyst - B2C in Germany.

This role sits at the intersection of category growth, marketing execution, and commercial analytics, supporting the scaling of a fast-moving multi-category marketplace across multiple European markets. You will transform demand-side insights, pricing signals, and performance data into actionable recommendations that directly influence growth decisions. Acting as a key connector between marketing, supply, and category teams, you will ensure alignment across priorities, campaigns, and commercial execution. The role is highly operational and analytical, requiring someone who can move between strategy and execution with ease. You will also own the end-to-end execution of promotional mechanics such as voucher campaigns while continuously optimizing performance and ROI. This is a high-impact position where your work will directly shape category expansion, campaign efficiency, and marketplace performance. It is ideal for someone who thrives in fast-paced environments and enjoys turning complex data into structured execution.

Accountabilities


Support multi-category growth by turning demand signals, pricing data, and user behavior insights into actionable growth recommendations.
Own and execute promotional campaigns end-to-end, including voucher setup, monitoring, optimization, and ROI tracking.
Align marketing and supply-side teams by consolidating forecasts, stock availability, pricing inputs, and performance priorities.
Ensure smooth execution of brand and partner campaigns by maintaining KPI tracking and data readiness.
Translate category strategies into performance marketing execution across channels such as Search, Paid Social, and Affiliates.
Build dashboards, tools, and documentation to improve operational efficiency and enable self-serve reporting across teams.
Identify automation and process improvements to reduce manual work and scale operational workflows.
Support cross-functional business reviews with clear insights and structured analysis.


Requirements


2-5 years of experience in e-commerce, category management, marketing operations, performance marketing, or commercial analytics.
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to structure complex data into clear, actionable insights.
Experience working with multiple stakeholders and managing parallel workstreams in fast-paced environments.
Highly organized, detail-oriented, and operationally strong with excellent execution discipline.
Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate across marketing, supply, and commercial teams.
Proactive, problem-solving mindset with a focus on ownership and delivery.
Familiarity with performance marketing channels such as Search, Shopping, Paid Social, or Affiliates is a plus.
Experience in marketplace environments or consumer electronics is an advantage.


Benefits


Competitive salary aligned with experience.
Fully remote-first working model with strong flexibility.
Workation opportunities of up to two months per year.
Learning and development budget for courses, conferences, and professional growth.
Access to mental health and personal development support programs.
Collaborative, feedback-driven culture focused on autonomy and impact.
Regular internal workshops and cross-team learning initiatives.
Office weeks and optional in-person team gatherings several times per year.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

description_full:
You'll conduct in-depth research on global flight markets, industry trends, and passenger demographics to provide valuable market intelligence and trend analysis to stakeholders.
You’ll monitor competitor activities across routes, analyzing fare structures, schedule changes, and promotional campaigns to maintain TUI's competitive positioning.
Screen capacity developments from airlines across markets and provide to Analytics function within Core services.
We'll count on you to screen and forecast capacity needs by monitoring airline schedule changes, new route announcements, and market developments to provide insights to our Analytics teams.
Conducting competitor analysis will be essential as you evaluate offerings, pricing models, route networks, and market positioning to identify competitive advantages and areas for improvement.
You'll provide data-driven recommendations for route development and optimisation, suggesting pricing strategies and identifying opportunities to enhance customer experience.
You’ll develop and maintain comprehensive dashboards and reports tracking key performance indicators including route developments, airline flight plan analysis, capacity reports over all airports and markets.
Collaborating with data analysts will help you interpret performance metrics and translate complex data into actionable business insights.
Designing and implementing research methodologies will address specific business questions and support strategic decision-making across the organisation.


About You


You have experience in market research, ideally within the airline or travel sector.
Your quantitative and qualitative analysis abilities enable you to work with large datasets and derive meaningful insights.
Proficiency in tools such as Excel, SPSS, Tableau, or equivalent analytics software supports your analytical work.
Knowledge of the global aviation market and airline industry dynamics provides context for your research.
Analytical thinking and problem-solving abilities help you tackle complex challenges effectively.
Communication abilities with capacity to present complex data clearly ensure your insights are understood by stakeholders.
Experience in designing and implementing research methodologies demonstrates your research expertise.
You work effectively with key stakeholders including commercial teams, analytics functions, and product development teams.


From a workplace to a place to belong. At TUI we embrace diversity, equity, and inclusion, encouraging everyone to come as you are, because together, our potential is limitless.

We are committed to supporting candidates with disabilities and impairments so if you require any support, please do let us know.

description_full:
For over 100 years, Deutsche Grammophon has been one of the world’s most prestigious and influential music companies: We are the market leader in classical music. Our artists include stars such as Anne-Sophie Mutter, Anna Netrebko, Daniel Barenboim, and Rolando Villazón, as well as promising newcomers like Daniil Trifonov, Alice Sara Ott, and Camille Thomas. We also represent the outstanding artists and catalogs of the Decca, Mercury Classics, and ECM labels.

Analytics, big data and cloud technologies are essential to our business. In the world of digital distribution channels and social media, we have access to more information than ever before. In the area of data analytics, we conduct in-depth analyses to better understand the listening behaviour of music fans and thus optimally market our artists and improve the listening experience. The findings of our analysis have strategic implications, such as the engagement of talented artists, the design of marketing activities and the allocation of resources.

We are seeking a highly analytical and problem solver-oriented individual to join our team for our streaming platform Stage+ . The successful candidate will be responsible for analyzing marketing campaigns and product data to provide insights and recommendations that will drive business growth and improve customer experience. The ideal candidate will be able to work cross-functionally with product and marketing teams, using data that will have a direct impact on the strategy of Stage+.

Here’s your chance to join! Effective immediately, we’re looking for a Product & Marketing Analyst (all genders) to join us at our Headquarters in Berlin. It will be a permanent full-time position.

Your Gig


Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to analyze large data sets, identify trends and patterns, and provide insights and recommendations to both product and marketing teams
Develop and implement tracking concepts in our website and apps (mobile and TV)
Conduct A/B tests and experiments to optimize user experience on Stage+; as well as A/B tests for marketing campaigns across different channels
You work closely in the product development process, supporting the definition of success criteria and conduct analyses to support hypothesis
Stay closely to the marketing team, conducting analysis of marketing campaigns to evaluate their effectiveness and ROI, identify areas for improvement, and recommend optimizations to drive performance
Develop and maintain dashboards and reports to track and measure the success of Stage+'s KPIs
Stay up to date with the latest trends and technologies in marketing and product analytics and provide recommendations on how to incorporate them into our processes


We Are Keen To Jam With You If You


Have a Bachelor's degree in a relevant field such as Marketing Analytics, Statistics, Data Science, or Business Administration; a master's degree is a plus
You gained at least 2-3 years of experience in product analytics and marketing analytics, preferably in a consumer-facing industry (ideally in a platform / app / subscription-based environment)
You are experienced with Google Tag Manager, Mixpanel, Adjust and data visualization tools
You understand basics in finance to match performance data to our business success metrics
Strong project management skills with the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously and deliver high-quality results on time
Knowledge of SQL, Python and Clarisights are a plus for this role
You feel confident and comfortable reading, writing, and speaking English; German skills are a plus but the willingness is to learn the language is a must


Your Personality Matters


You have excellent communication and collaboration skills with the ability to work effectively with cross-functional teams
You feel most comfortable working within a dynamic environment and enjoy working towards a common goal together with interdisciplinary stakeholders
You value your excellent abilities in analytical thinking and out-of-the-box thinking
Your positivity and charisma inspire the people around you and enable you to transfer your knowledge in a highly engaging manner
You thrive working in a team that shares a growth- as well as hands-on-mindset and you would choose teamwork over heroism anytime


Our Values, Our Compass, Our Culture


Creativity – Home to Artists, Innovators and Entrepreneurs.
Connection – We are ONE team.
Passion – Music is our first love.
Responsibility – It’s up to us.
Diversity – Music is universal.
Factuition – Guided by head AND heart.


Our Benefits – Because You’re Important To Us


Feel the music! – Thanks to a concert ticket budget, streaming service vouchers, employee discounts on physical music and merchandise products as well as various internal events
Enjoy flexibility! – Thanks to our hybrid work model you benefit from high flexibility and room for collaboration and interactions in our headquarter in Berlin
Time to relax! – You are more than your job, benefit from 30 vacation days plus fixed company closure days
Always on track! - Keep moving sustainably through the capital thanks to special rates for BVG tickets (public transport) and our job bike leasing offer
Develop yourself! - We offer professional development through various workshops and courses
Self-care matters! – We promote and support mental health with our Employee Assistance Program for all challenges in life (professional and private); we offer confidential counseling and coaching through a third-party provider


#joinuniversalmusic

We look forward to receiving your application via our online application form! Please upload your documents (letter of motivation / cover letter, CV, references) as files and state your earliest starting date as well as salary expectations.

MUSIC IS UNIVERSAL!

As a large and globally operating company, Universal Music promotes diversity and opposes discrimination. We aspire to reflect the diversity represented by our artists through their music and individuality in our teams. We pledge ourselves to be an equal opportunity employer and enable individuals to express themselves – regardless of age, sex, gender, appearance, origin, faith, physical/mental ability, or sexual orientation.

If you are unsure about submitting an application because, for example, you do not fulfil all of the requirements, we would like to encourage you to try it nonetheless!

#YOUatUMG

description_full:
About HOLY


HOLY is the soft drink revolution: With our powdered drinks HOLY Energy®, HOLY Hydration®, HOLY Iced Tea®, and HOLY Milkshake® you get amazing taste across 50+ flavors while maintaining a good conscience—for your body & the environment.
Over 2M loyal customers: Started in a shared apartment by three best friends, we have scaled to Europe's fastest growing consumer brand—and our cult-like community, the HOLY Squad, loves it: 4.6 stars on reviews.io speak for themselves.
From D2C to Global Omnichannel: In less than four years, we've profitably scaled HOLY to more than €150M D2C revenue in DACH, FR, UK and PL. Now scaling our retail business, we're on an exciting transition to becoming a true omnichannel rocketship.
We are still just getting started: Backed by the world's best consumer investors, we aim to revolutionize the soft drink industry and build a generational love brand: tastier, healthier & more sustainable. Our team of 150+ people aims high (Coca-Cola, we're coming for you!).


About The Position

As a Marketing Analyst at HOLY, you'll be the analytical backbone of our marketing and growth teams. Your mission: Turn data into decisions by delivering accurate forecasts, actionable insights, and performance analysis that fuel HOLY's commercial engine.

Responsibilities:

Sales Forecasting & Budget Tracking


Own weekly revenue forecasts and maintain our driver-based forecasting model to keep expectations aligned with reality.
Track marketing spend and budget pacing, ensuring optimal allocation across channels and campaigns.
Partner with Finance and Growth teams to optimize HOLY's commercial planning and resource allocation.


Ad-hoc Analytics & Campaign Performance Insights


Deliver rapid-response analysis for marketing teams, including campaign debriefs, performance deep dives, and channel troubleshooting.
Build and maintain dashboards that empower teams to make faster, smarter decisions
Turn complex data into clear, actionable recommendations that drive ROI and customer acquisition efficiency.


Incrementality & Marketing Channel Analytics


Design and execute incrementality tests to measure true marketing effectiveness across channels.
Provide data-driven recommendations to improve marketing ROI and customer acquisition strategy.
Analyze channel performance, identify optimization opportunities, and support strategic decision-making.


Order Volume Forecasting


Build and maintain short-term demand forecasts that factor in promotional impact and offering changes.
Model campaign volume scenarios to support inventory planning and fulfillment operations.
Help optimize resource allocation across the business by connecting marketing activity to operational needs.


Cross-Functional Operating Cadence


Prepare weekly and monthly performance meetings with clear, compelling analysis that supports management decisions.
Collaborate closely with Marketing, CRM, Finance, E-Commerce, and Operations teams.
Proactively flag risks, spot opportunities, and drive follow-through on your recommendations.


About You


Customer and business intuition: Connecting data to HOLY's product, customer behavior, and business model comes naturally, supported by 2–4 years of hands-on experience in analytics, BI, or data science (ideally in e-commerce, D2C, or consumer goods).
Analytical rigor meets speed: A strong 80/20 mindset helps you balance depth and pace, delivering fast and reliable analysis without unnecessary complexity. You have a proven ability to build forecasting models, run incrementality testing, and turn performance data into actionable insights.
Technical proficiency: Proficiency in SQL and advanced spreadsheet modeling (Google Sheets or Excel) is expected, along with experience in BI tools like Metabase, Looker, or Tableau. Bonus points for Python or R.
Clear communicator: Complex insights are translated into crisp, compelling stories for different audiences and stakeholders. Fluency in English is required.
Ownership mindset: A proactive approach to spotting issues, flagging them early, and driving recommendations through to action is essential. You do not wait to be asked twice.


Our Offering

In today's job market, A-players have many options to choose from. As part of our offer, we prioritise providing a productive working environment and opportunities for growth. In addition to competitive compensation, including company shares, you can expect:


A successful and growing company: Join a company that has been growing profitable and quickly for the last 36+ months and have raised a series B, while many other e-commerce companies are struggling.
Huge impact: Enjoy huge impact to shape HOLY’s internationalisation in a top market.
Awesome team: Join an awesome team of colleagues who are ambitious, funny, down-to-earth, and authentic. Get to know us and see for yourself!
Top product: Marketing is important, but a good product is essential. Our differentiated soft drinks have won over 2.5 million customers. Check out our reviews.io reviews or contact us for free samples.
Startup culture: Join a startup culture that values flat hierarchies, fast pace, and personal development. With us, you can make a real impact.
Perfect setup: Enjoy a perfect setup that includes all the requirements of a modern employer, such as a central office in Berlin Prenzlauerberg, flexible working hours, and much more.
Competitive comp. + equity: Join us at a pivotal stage of hypergrowth. We offer a strong salary and meaningful equity—giving you the chance to share in our current success and benefit from the massive upside as we scale.
How we support your growth: flexible hours, 28 vacation days, 30 work-from-abroad days, €1,500 learning budget, Urban Sports Club membership, public transport subsidy, and biannual 360° feedback tied to your compensation.


Find out more about HOLY and our team on our careers page.

The Process

As we are experiencing rapid growth and have ambitious plans, the optimal start date for the position is today. Nonetheless, we are prepared to patiently await the arrival of the ideal candidate, recognizing that quality ultimately yields the best results.


Getting to know you: You will have a 30 minute screening interview with Eric (Talent Acquisition Manager), where we will talk to you more about HOLY and the position and see if our expectations match.
Hiring Manager interview with Martijn: Diving deeper into some more technical topics and your experience with Martijn our Sr. Business Operations & Analytics, our Sr. Talent Development Manager.
Case study & on-site interview: As soon as you have successfully completed the first interviews, you will receive a case study. In a second meeting at our office, the team will discuss your case study with you and answer questions about our workspace, the HOLY Temple.
Founders Interview: As a strong cultural fit is important to us, you will have a final meeting with Phil , one of our HOLY Founders. This gives both you and us the opportunity for a final check.
Job offer and decision: If everything goes well, you will receive an offer and we are ready to embark you this journey together with you!


Typically his process should take around 2 weeks.

Any questions or concerns?

If you have any questions or feedback, please send an email to jobs@holy.com.

10405, Berlin

description_full:
Role Overview:

AVATR is looking for a Product, Market & User Research Manager (Europe) to join our growing team in Munich. This role will lead the European product, market and user research team, be fully responsible for formulating and implementing the overall research strategy for the European region, oversee in-depth research on overseas (focusing on European) market product status, collect and analyse dealer and user feedback, track regional competitor dynamics and regulatory changes, output cost-effective overseas product configuration lists and professional research reports, and . The role will also be responsible for team management, capacity building and cross-departmental resource coordination to ensure the efficient operation of the research team and the effective landing of research results.



What You Will Do:


Team Leadership & Management: Lead the European product, market and user research team, formulate teamwork plans and performance indicators, be responsible for the recruitment, training, assessment and development of team members, build a professional research team, and improve the overall professional ability and work efficiency of the team.
Overall Research Strategy Formulation & Implementation: Develop the overall product, market and user research strategy for the European region and phased implementation plans, clarify research priorities and directions, integrate research resources, and ensure that the research work is aligned with the company's overall development strategy and business needs.
Market & Product Research Oversight: Oversee in-depth research on overseas (focusing on European) market product status, collect and sort out feedback from dealers and end-users, track regional competitor dynamics, product configuration plans and core selling points, establish a systematic research database, and provide comprehensive market and product insights.
Product Configuration & Regulatory Research: Lead the team to analyse regional preferences for specific product configurations (value perception) and relevant regulatory requirements on configurations in different European regions, output overseas product configuration lists that maximize value while controlling costs, and provide support for product optimization and compliance.
Competitor & Market Segment Research: Focus on the research of European regional markets, oversee the monitoring and analysis of competitor business dynamics, market product competition situation, tariff policies, local policies and regulatory changes, research and analyse the scale of segmented markets and competitor sales performance, and provide data support for market positioning and business decision-making.
Research Report Output & Strategy Support: Oversee the sorting, analysis and induction of research data, guide the team to output high-quality regular and special research reports (including product positioning analysis, competitor selection analysis, pricing analysis, EAP, EATP, etc.) and provide professional suggestions and strategic support for cross-departmental decision-making.
Cross-departmental Collaboration & Resource Coordination: Act as the core contact for European product, market and user research work, cooperate closely with product, marketing, design, sales, legal and other cross-functional teams, coordinate resources, promote the effective application of research results in product development, market promotion and other links, and solve cross-departmental research-related needs.
Research System Optimization: Continuously optimize the European product, market and user research methods, processes and tools, promote the innovation of research models, improve the accuracy, efficiency and pertinence of research work, and adapt to the dynamic changes of the European market.




What You Will Bring:


Bachelor's degree or above in Marketing, Market Research, Product Management, Business Administration or related majors; master's degree is preferred.
5-8 years of relevant work experience in product research, market research or user research in the automotive industry (preferably new energy vehicles), including at least 2-3 years of team management experience. Experience in the European market is a must, and familiar with the European automotive market ecology is an advantage.
Profound understanding of the European automotive market, including market rules, consumer demand, competitor patterns, relevant policies and regulations (such as vehicle safety, environmental protection, configuration compliance), and have rich experience in formulating targeted research strategies and outputting strategic research reports.
Rich experience in team management, able to effectively lead and motivate the team, formulate reasonable work plans, and promote the achievement of team goals; have good team building and talent training capabilities.
Proficient in using various market research methods and tools, able to independently design research plans, organize research implementation, sort out and analyse research data, and output professional and targeted research reports; familiar with EAP, EATP and other related analysis work is preferred.
Have excellent strategic thinking and analytical ability, be able to extract effective information from complex data and market dynamics, identify core problems and opportunities and put forward targeted strategic suggestions.
Excellent communication, coordination and negotiation skills, able to effectively cooperate with cross-functional teams, external partners (such as dealers, research institutions) and team members, and have good problem-solving ability.
Fluent in English (both written and spoken); proficient in Chinese is a plus.
Proactive, responsible, detail-oriented, able to work independently and bear certain work pressure, with a strong learning ability, adaptability and innovation awareness.


What We Offer:


Competitive Salary: Reflecting your skills and experience.
Hybrid Working: Flexibility to work both remotely and in the office.
Health & Wellness: Access to the Wellhub Pass for your well-being.
Employee Development: Learning opportunities and training programs to support your career growth.
Transport Support: Deutschland ticket or fuel card.
Meal Allowance: For lunch at the canteen.
Gifts: Celebratory gifts for work anniversaries and Chinese New Year.
Company Events: Regular team-building and social activities.
Optional Company Pension Scheme: Available based on your needs.
Inclusive Culture: A diverse, supportive environment where every voice is valued.


Thank you for considering joining our team! We look forward to hearing from you and hope you become a part of our company. If you are interested in the position, please apply now. We are excited about the possibility of working together to create a bright future!

Über Uns

Unsere Mission ist es, unsere Kunden in der kompletten Digital Customer Journey zu begleiten, zu unterstützen und diese kontinuierlich zu optimieren. Dafür setzen wir auf individuelle Maßnahmen, die jedem Berührungspunkt in der Customer Journey gerecht werden. Wir setzen dabei auf die Kombination von Content Marketing, SEO und Social Media und stehen Ihnen sowohl strategisch als auch operativ zur Seite.




ÜBER DIE IMG

Als Teil der The Relevance Group ist es unsere Mission, für unsere Kunden die bestmögliche digitale Customer Journey zu konzipieren, umzusetzen und kontinuierlich zu optimieren. Dabei hilft uns ein hochkompetentes Team, das die gesamte Klaviatur des digitalen Marketings beherrscht und mit Hilfe des interdisziplinären Know-hows kontinuierlich neue Ansätze entwickelt um unsere Kunden am Markt erfolgreich zu positionieren.

Deine Aufgaben

Mit einem Mix aus bewährten Consulting Methoden und Werkzeugen sowie einem Schuss Start-Up Mentalität kreieren wir gemeinsam Kundenerlebnisse, die den Menschen den größtmöglichen Mehrwert bringen. Als Organic Growth Analyst - SEO / GEO & AI (m/w/d) in Hamburg übernimmst Du bei uns die zentrale Verantwortung für:




Data Intelligence & Analytics Architecture: Du baust automatisierte Dashboards (Looker Studio, PowerBI), die über bloße Rankings hinausgehen und den gesamten Funnel bis zum Lead/Sale abbilden. Du nutzt Datenanalysen, um Business-Cases für SEO-Investments zu validieren.
Technisches Consulting & Architektur: Du erstellst tiefgreifende Tech Audits sowie PageSpeed Analysen und begleitest die technische Implementierung auf verschiedenen CMS-Typen (von Headless bis Enterprise). Du gibst fachlichen Input bei der Konzeption neuer technischer Module, um SEO-Anforderungen von Tag 1 an zu verankern.
Datenbasierte Analyse: Erstellung von fundierten SEO-Maßnahmenkatalogen, Keyword- und Relevanzanalysen sowie Wettbewerbsbetrachtungen, um Potenziale klar zu priorisieren.
End-to-End Projektmanagement: Eigenständige Planung und Steuerung von Kundenprojekten. Du hältst die Fäden in der Hand, koordinierst interne Teams (Content, Dev, Design) und sicherst die Qualität der Ergebnisse.
Monitoring & Impact: Du verwandelst Rohdaten in Insights. Du verantwortest das Reporting zentraler KPIs und optimierst Kampagnen basierend auf dem Business-Impact (Conversions/ROI), nicht nur auf Rankings.
Strategic Consulting: Beratung von Entscheidern auf Head-of- und C-Level-Ebene. Du erklärst komplexe technische Sachverhalte verständlich und leitest aus Daten klare Business-Empfehlungen ab.




Dein Profil

Wir suchen Menschen, keine Maschinen. Deswegen solltest du vor allem Spaß an deiner Arbeit haben. Ganz ohne Hard Skills geht es natürlich nicht. Um alle Herausforderungen zu meistern, wünschen wir uns von dir:

Ein erfolgreich abgeschlossenes Studium
3–5 Jahre fundierte Erfahrung im SEO-Bereich (Agentur oder Inhouse).
Nachweislicher Erfolg mit großen Domains und/oder gesättigten Märkten
Bestehende Routinen in der eigenständigen Projekt-/Kundenführung und Teamkoordination.
Sicherer Umgang mit SEO-Suites (GSC, Sistrix, Screaming Frog, SEMrush/Ahrefs)
Expertenwissen in Google Analytics 4, Google Tag Manager und der Verknüpfung von Datenquellen.
Ausgeprägte Leidenschaft für Datenvisualisierung und modernes Dashboarding.
Intuitive Nutzung von KI-Tools (OpenAI, Perplexity etc.) und hast Lust, die Zukunft der Suche (GEO) aktiv mitzugestalten
Präsentationsstark und verhandlungssicher in Deutsch und Englisch.
Umgang mit MS Office gehört für dich zum Standard
Strukturierte Arbeitsweise, Belastbarkeit und ein hohes Maß an Teamfähigkeit.
Schnelle Auffassungsgabe und Lust auf stetige Weiterentwicklung.
Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse (mind. C1 Level)

Wir suchen keinen reinen "SEO-Experten", sondern einen unternehmerisch denkenden Analyst, der SEO als Teil einer ganzheitlichen Marketing-Landschaft versteht. Du verbindest tiefes Fachwissen mit modernsten KI-Technologien und steuerst komplexe Projekte eigenverantwortlich zum Erfolg.




Damit sammelst du Pluspunkte:

Erfahrung mit BigQuery oder SQL zur Analyse großer Datensätze.
Kenntnisse in Server-Side Tracking.
Erfahrung in der Attributionsmodellierung.
Erfahrungen mit die Nutzung von APIs von SEO Tools
Gute JavaScript-Kenntnisse – du hast einen Überblick über moderne Frameworks und deren Auswirkungen auf SEO; Code musst du nicht selbst schreiben können, kannst ihn aber lesen und z. B. Loading-Hints eigenständig bewerten.
Erfahrung im Bereich Frontend Entwicklung
Erfahrungen im Bereich UX/UI Gestaltung und/oder Optimierung




Dein Extra

• Flexible Arbeitsgestaltung und Vertrauensarbeitszeitt

• Neueste Technologien und Know-how

• 30 Tage Urlaub + frei an deinem Geburtstag

description_full:
Position Overview

Über den Bereich

In der Privatkundenbank bündelt die Deutsche Bank ihre Expertise im Privatkundengeschäft in einem Geschäftsbereich – mit den Marken Deutsche Bank und Postbank als Marktführer in Deutschland und einem globalen Netzwerk, die das Geschäft der Bank mit vermögenden Kund*innen weltweit sowie mit Privatkund*innen und kleinen bis mittelgroßen Unternehmen umfasst. In ihrem Heimatmarkt Deutschland wie auch international bietet die Privatkundenbank ihren Kund*innen hochwertige Finanzdienstleistungen aus einer Hand – von umfassenden Angeboten für Privatkund*innen über die Betreuung anspruchsvoller Unternehmer und ihrer Familien bis hin zu Lösungen für kleinere und mittelgroße Unternehmen in wichtigen Märkten der Eurozone. Die Privatkundenbank ist neben der Unternehmensbank , der Investmentbank und dem Asset Management mit ihrem Vermögensverwalter DWS eine der vier Geschäftsbereiche des Deutsche-Bank-Konzerns. Die Deutsche Bank ist Deutschlands führende Bank mit globalem Netzwerk und fest verwurzelt in Europa.

Du bist für den Bereich Marketing Daily Banking (Konto, Karte, Einlagen und Digitale Services) und Marketing Experiences (Kooperationen) für die Privatkundenbank (Deutschland) verantwortlich.

Deine Aufgaben


Du setzt den Maßstab für Performance – definierst, konsolidierst und governst ein ganzheitliches KPI‑Framework für Media, Web und CX und verankerst es wirksam in allen Marketingprozessen.
Du schaffst Transparenz, die steuert – entwickelst aussagekräftige Dashboards und Reportings, die die Performance verständlich machen und fundierte Entscheidungen ermöglichen.
Du verwandelst Daten in echte Insights – analysierst Kampagnen‑, Channel‑ und Journey‑Performance und bereitest klare Readouts für Marketing‑ und Sales‑Stakeholder auf.
Du bist der Go‑to‑Partner für Performance-Fragen – agierst als zentrale Anlaufstelle für KPI‑ und Messlogiken und arbeitest eng mit Media, CX und Data Analytics zusammen.
Du denkst Performance einen Schritt voraus – nutzt Predictive und KI‑gestützte Analytics (z. B. Forecasting, Segmentierung, Anomalieerkennung), um Trends frühzeitig zu erkennen und Optimierungen zu priorisieren.
Du berätst auf Augenhöhe – unterstützt Marketing Manager bei KPI‑Definition, Messlogik und Performance‑Interpretation und etablierst value‑basierte Steuerung (z. B. CLV) als Entscheidungsgrundlage.



Deine Fähigkeiten und Erfahrungen


Du hast ein abgeschlossenes Hochschulstudium, idealerweise mit Schwerpunkt Marketing, Medien, Kommunikation oder Wirtschaft.
Du bringst nachweisliche Erfahrung in Marketing‑, Media‑ und Web‑Analytics mit und steuerst sowie interpretierst Performance‑KPIs sicher im Marketingkontext.
Du gehst routiniert mit Datenanalyse, Segmentierung und Forecasting um – inklusive Search‑ und Paid‑Logiken sowie modellbasierter Prognosen.
Du verfügst über starke Expertise in Data Visualization & BI und entwickelst aussagekräftige, stakeholdergerechte Dashboards (z. B. Power BI oder Tableau).
Du setzt Predictive und KI‑gestützte Analytics praxisnah ein, um Insights zu skalieren – etwa durch automatisierte Auswertungen, Pattern Detection oder Prognosen.
Du verstehst CLV‑Modelle und Werttreiberlogiken und nutzt sie, um Marketingmaßnahmen daten‑ und value‑basiert zu priorisieren.
Du überzeugst durch analytische Stärke, Präzision und Ergebnisorientierung, kommunizierst klar, übersetzt Daten in handlungsrelevante Empfehlungen, arbeitest gern im Team – und verfügst über sehr gute Deutsch‑ und Englischkenntnisse.



Was wir Dir bieten

Wir bieten eine breite Palette von Leistungen, die all Ihre beruflichen und persönlichen Bedürfnisse abdecken.


Emotional ausgeglichen Eine positive Haltung hilft uns, die Herausforderungen des Alltags zu meistern – beruflich wie privat. Profitieren Sie von Angeboten wie Beratung in schwierigen Lebenssituationen und Angeboten zur Förderung mentaler Gesundheit.
Körperlich fit Mit Angeboten zur Aufrechterhaltung Ihrer persönlichen Gesundheit und einem förderlichen beruflichen Umfeld hilft Ihnen die Bank, körperlich fit zu bleiben. Profitieren Sie von Angeboten wie umfangreichen Check-up Untersuchungen, Impfangeboten und Beratung zur gesunden Lebensführung.
Sozial vernetzt Der Austausch mit anderen eröffnet uns neue Perspektiven, bringt uns beruflich wie persönlich voran und stärkt unser Selbstvertrauen und Wohlbefinden. Profitieren Sie von Angeboten wie Unterstützung durch den pme Familienservice, das FitnessCenter Job, flexible Arbeitszeitmodelle (bspw. Teilzeit, Jobtandem, hybrides Arbeiten) sowie einer umfangreichen Kultur der Vielfalt, Chancengleichheit und Teilhabe.
Finanziell abgesichert Die Bank sichert Sie nicht nur während Ihrer aktiven Karriere, sondern auch für die Zukunft finanziell ab und unterstützt Ihre Flexibilität sowie Mobilität – egal ob privat oder beruflich. Profitieren Sie von Angeboten wie Beitragsplänen für Altersvorsorge, Bankdienstleistungen für Mitarbeiter*innen, Firmenfahrrad oder dem Deutschlandticket.



Da die Benefits je nach Standort geringfügig variieren, gehe bitte bei konkreten Fragen auf Deinen Recruiter / Deine Recruiterin zu.

Die Stelle wird in Voll- und in Teilzeit angeboten.

Bei Fragen zum Rekrutierungsprozess steht Dir Kerstin Nörber gerne zur Verfügung.

Kontakt Kerstin Nörber: +49 ( 0) 69-910-22155

Wir streben eine Unternehmenskultur an, in der wir gemeinsam jeden Tag das Beste geben. Dazu gehören verantwortungsvolles Handeln, wirtschaftliches Denken, Initiative ergreifen und zielgerichtete Zusammenarbeit.

Gemeinsam teilen und feiern wir die Erfolge unserer Mitarbeiter*innen. Gemeinsam sind wir die Deutsche Bank Gruppe.

Wir begrüßen Bewerbungen von allen Menschen und fördern ein positives, faires und integratives Arbeitsumfeld.

description_full:
About Wolt

At Wolt, we create technology that brings joy, simplicity and earnings to the neighborhoods of the world. In 2014 we started with delivery of restaurant food. Now we’re building the delivery of (almost) everything and you’ll find us in over 500 cities in 30 countries around the world. In 2022 we joined forces with DoorDash and together we keep on dreaming big and expanding across the globe.

Working at Wolt isn’t always easy, but it’s definitely exciting. Here you’ll learn more, build more, and ship more than in most other companies. You’ll be challenged a lot, but also have a lot of fun on the way. So, if you’re a self-starter with drive and entrepreneurial spirit, this could be the ride of your life.

Are you passionate about supporting business management through sharp financial analysis? Do you have solid experience crunching data and would like to work in one of the fastest growing tech companies in Europe? If so, then please read on!

About The Team

The Marketing Finance & Strategy team is part of Wolt's Finance & Strategy organization, sitting within the Marketplace & Consumer line of business. We own the budget, forecasting, and efficiency tracking for Wolt's entire marketing investment - spanning Discounts & Promos, Customer Acquisition, and Brand Marketing across 22+ countries and three business lines (Marketplace, New Verticals, Commerce Platform). We work closely with Marketing leadership, regional teams, and DoorDash counterparts to make sure every euro of marketing spend is tracked, forecasted, and evaluated.

We're also one of the most AI-forward teams in Wolt F&S - Cursor, Claude Code, Glean, and custom automation are part of how we work every day.

About The Role

We're looking for a Senior Analyst to own the operational and technical backbone of our team. You'll be responsible for our central Marketing Forecasting Tool (MFT), the monthly closing process, weekly forecast uploads, and the data pipeline that powers our Global Marketing Weekly Business Review. Beyond keeping the engine running, your first major mission is to fix the data foundation: reconciling sources, aligning budget versions, and standardizing metric definitions so that all our numbers tie across every document and system.

This is a role with real end-to-end ownership. You won't just support the process - you'll run it.

What you’ll be doing


Own the MFT end-to-end: a 30-country forecasting tool that is the central hub for all marketing budget data at Wolt
Run the monthly closing process for both Discounts & Promos and Customer Acquisition Cost, including actuals validation with regional teams
Execute weekly forecast uploads to our management reporting systems (Pulse/IBM)
Build and maintain the data pipeline behind the Global Marketing WBR (Snowflake pulls, OKR updates, data QA)
Be the first point of contact for budget questions from marketing teams across the organization
Fix known data inconsistencies between our reporting tools and create a single source of truth
Build new analytical frameworks (Payback/LTV, M2 Retention tracking) from scratch
Work with AI tools (Cursor, Glean) from day one to automate and improve how the team operates


Our humble expectations


Have 3-5 years of experience in FP&A, management accounting, or finance operations, ideally at a tech company or fast-scaling marketplace
Are advanced in Excel / Google Sheets and can confidently work in complex, multi-tab models
Have hands-on SQL / Snowflake experience and can write queries, build data pulls, and maintain pipelines
Have owned a forecasting or budgeting tool end-to-end before - not just contributed, but ran it
Are comfortable with month-end closing processes: actuals validation, accruals, variance analysis
Spot when data sources don't tie - and can't let it go until you've found the root cause
Are fluent in English


Nice to haves:


Experience with Python or Google App Scripts for automation
Familiarity with marketing finance (CAC, CPA, D&P, unit economics)
Interest in AI-assisted workflows - we'll teach you the tools
German language skills
Experience in a multi-country or multi-entity finance environment


Please note that we do not accept applications sent by mail. You should submit your application through our careers website in English!

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

We’re committed to growing and empowering a more inclusive community within our company, industry, and cities. That’s why we hire and cultivate diverse teams of people from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We believe that true innovation happens when everyone has room at the table and the tools, resources, and opportunity to excel.

Deine Rolle

Du übernimmst die volle Verantwortung für unser Email Marketing und entwickelst es auf das nächste Level: strategisch, technisch und operativ. Du arbeitest direkt an unseren Kern-Funnels: Ticket Sales, Exhibitor Leads und Retention.

Dabei bist du nicht nur Umsetzer, sondern Treiber.




Deine Aufgaben

Verantwortung für alle Email Marketing Aktivitäten über unsere Summits hinweg
Aufbau, Optimierung und Skalierung von CRM- und Email-Workflows
Entwicklung von performanten Kampagnen entlang der gesamten Customer Journey (Lead → Conversion → Retention)
Operative Umsetzung von Kampagnen (Segmentierung, Versand, Testing, Reporting)
Weiterentwicklung unserer Automatisierungen (Lifecycle, Nurturing, Re-Engagement, Upsell)
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Sales, Marketing und Operations
Verantwortung für Datenqualität, Segmentierung und CRM-Struktur
Analyse und kontinuierliche Optimierung relevanter KPIs (Open Rate, CTR, Conversion, Revenue Contribution)
Entwicklung neuer Ideen, Formate und Growth-Ansätze im Email & CRM Marketing




Ein Fokusprojekt: HubSpot Integration

Wir befinden uns aktuell in einer Übergangsphase.

Du wirst:

Die Einführung und Integration von HubSpot aktiv begleiten
Bestehende Systeme (u.a. Spotler, Instantly.ai) sinnvoll integrieren bzw. konsolidieren
Saubere Datenstrukturen und skalierbare Prozesse entwickeln
Sicherstellen, dass Marketing, Sales und CRM sauber zusammenspielen




Was du mitbringst

Mindestens ein Jahr Erfahrung im Email Marketing / CRM Marketing, idealerweise im B2B Umfeld (B2B Erfahrung ist kein "must-have")
Starke Hands-on Mentalität: du setzt Dinge selbst um und denkst nicht nur konzeptionell
Erfahrung mit Marketing Automation Tools (idealerweise HubSpot)
Verständnis für Customer Journeys, Funnel-Logiken und Conversion-Optimierung
Sehr gutes Gefühl für Zielgruppen-Kommunikation und Copy
Technisches Verständnis (Daten, APIs, Tracking, Integrationen)

description_full:
Stellenbeschreibung:




Rügamer & Steiner ist die führende E-Mail Marketing Agentur hinter den erfolgreichsten E-Commerce Unternehmen in DE, AU & CH.




Über 100+ Online Shops vertrauen uns bereits und erreichen mit uns die Herzen & Geldbeutel ihrer Kunden.




Da immer mehr Kunden ihr Vertrauen in Rügamer & Steiner setzen suchen wir Dich als Account Manager CRM & E-Mail Marketing (m/w/d).




Du bist die strategisch führende Rolle in der Kundenbetreuung.




Du verantwortest die ganzheitliche Steuerung, die Weiterentwicklung und den langfristigen Erfolg der zugewiesenen Kundenaccounts.




Du bist der primäre Ansprechpartner für alle strategischen Entscheidungen und stellst sicher, dass die Kundenbeziehung stabil, wachstumsorientiert und nachhaltig aufgebaut wird.


Aufgaben


Deine Aufgabenbereiche:





Strategische Betreuung: Entwicklung und Planung fundierter Konzepte und langfristiger Maßnahmen, die auf die Geschäftsziele der Kunden einzahlen.


Kundenführung: Führung und Durchführung von regelmäßigen Calls, um Strategien zu präsentieren, Fortschritte zu besprechen und die Kundenbindung zu stärken.


Account-Entwicklung & Upselling: Proaktive Identifikation von Wachstumspotenzialen beim Kunden, Vorbereitung und eigenständige Durchführung von Upsells.


Maßnahmenplanung: Abstimmung und Planung von größeren Rollouts und neuen Kampagnen in Zusammenarbeit mit dem Kunden.


Marktexpertise: Kontinuierliche Beobachtung von Marketing-Trends, um diese proaktiv in die Kundenstrategien zu integrieren.





Du trägst die Verantwortung für die ganzheitliche Entwicklung und Skalierung der Kundenaccounts, sowie für die strategische Zielerreichung und langfristige Kundenzufriedenheit. Zusätzlich stellst du sicher, dass operative Exzellenz erreicht wird und du perspektivisch operative Aufgaben gezielt an Projektmanager delegieren kannst.




Wichtiger Hinweis zur Einarbeitung:




In der Anfangsphase betreust du Kunden ganzheitlich und übernimmst auch die operative Umsetzung. Du musst alle operativen Prozesse und Aufgaben (Projektmanagement) vollständig erlernen und beherrschen. Dies ist zwingend erforderlich, um Kunden anfänglich eigenständig zu betreuen und später operative Aufgaben gezielt und kompetent an Projektmanager delegieren zu können.




Perfekt für dich, wenn du bereits Erfahrung in der strategischen Kundenbetreuung hast und weißt, wie wichtig Weitblick, Verantwortung und nachhaltiges Wachstum sind.


Qualifikation


Das solltest du sein:




➜ Du hast fundierte Erfahrung im Bereich Kundenmanagement, Account Management oder strategischer Beratung




➜ Du denkst strategisch, konzeptionell und unternehmerisch




➜ Du bist kommunikationsstark, verhandlungssicher und hast eine hohe Vertriebsaffinität (Upselling)




➜ Du arbeitest strukturiert, lösungsorientiert und eigenverantwortlich




➜ Du kannst komplexe Inhalte klar und überzeugend vermitteln




➜ Du bist bereit, dich tief in operative Prozesse, Tools und Projektmanagement einzuarbeiten


Benefits


Was wir dir bieten:





Sicherer Arbeitsplatz: Jede Person, die wir einstellen soll langfristig mit uns wachsen. Das Ziel jeder Einstellung ist eine unbefristete Übernahme.


Tolle Arbeitsatmosphäre: Der Umgang untereinander ist sehr kollegial und familiär. Wir ziehen alle am gleichen Strang und motivieren uns gegenseitig Bestleistungen zu erzielen und persönlich zu wachsen. Es gibt zudem ein monatliches Team-Lunch auf Kosten der Firma.


Gute Vergütung & Karrierechancen: Unsere Mitarbeiter: innen erhalten eine faire Vergütung und vermögenswirksame Leistungen. Über die nächsten Jahre werden wir weiter wachsen und expandieren. Daher benötigen wir weitere Experten und/oder Führungskräfte





...und noch viel mehr:





Modernes Büro in zentraler Lage in Düsseldorf Pempelfort mit Dachterasse und Anschluss an ÖPNV


Stetige Weiterbildung & Karriere


Zusammenhalt in einem dynamischen und kollegialen Team


Ein attraktives Gehaltsmodell


Monatliches Teamessen, Firmen-Events, Messen, Vorträge und Veranstaltungen


Sehr gute Arbeitsmittel, beste Hardware von Apple, höhenverstellbare Tische





Du erkennst Dich wieder und möchtest mit uns ins Gespräch kommen?




Dann bewirb dich noch heute und werde schon bald ein Teil des Teams!

Projektmanagement Kundenbetreuung Accountmanagement

description_full:

title: Junior Product Marketing Manager
description_snippet:
Join us as Junior Product Marketing Manager on our existing and upcoming games!

At Twin Harbour Interactive we strive to develop high-quality strategy games and make them accessible for all players around the world. In our eyes game development is a promise for fun and entertainment – that’s what we live and aim for!




As the Junior Product Marketing Manager, your mission is to support the execution and scaling of product marketing initiatives across our portfolio, with a strong focus on player-facing messaging, app store presence, and live feature communication.

You will play a hands-on role in shaping how our games show up in the app stores and at key product moments, ensuring clear, consistent, and impactful messaging across Store In-App Events, feature launches, and live-ops beats.
description_full:
Join us as Junior Product Marketing Manager on our existing and upcoming games!

At Twin Harbour Interactive we strive to develop high-quality strategy games and make them accessible for all players around the world. In our eyes game development is a promise for fun and entertainment – that’s what we live and aim for!




As the Junior Product Marketing Manager, your mission is to support the execution and scaling of product marketing initiatives across our portfolio, with a strong focus on player-facing messaging, app store presence, and live feature communication.

You will play a hands-on role in shaping how our games show up in the app stores and at key product moments, ensuring clear, consistent, and impactful messaging across Store In-App Events, feature launches, and live-ops beats.

You will own Store In-App Events end-to-end and support broader go-to-market execution in close collaboration with Product, Publishing, Growth, and Creative teams.

This role reports to the Head of Product Marketing.




YOUR MISSION

Store In-App Events Ownership

Own the end-to-end execution of Store In-App Events across platforms (Apple App Store & Google Play)
Plan, create, and publish Store In-App Events aligned with product updates, live-ops beats, and promotions
Partner with Product, Live Ops, and Creative teams to define messaging, timing, and visual requirements
Ensure Store In-App Events reflect product priorities, player motivations, and seasonal beats
Track performance and extract learnings to improve visibility, engagement, and conversion continuously

GTM & Launch Support

Support GTM execution for features, live-ops events, and content updates
Prepare briefs, timelines, checklists, and internal documentation for launches
Ensure messaging is consistently applied across store, UA, and community touchpoints
Coordinate inputs and approvals across cross-functional teams
Support launch retrospectives and documentation of best practices

Messaging & Brand Consistency Support

Apply approved messaging frameworks, narratives, and brand guidelines across executions
Support copywriting and adaptation of player-facing messaging for store and product beats
Help maintain internal documentation for product positioning, personas, and narratives
Flag inconsistencies or execution risks early to the Head of Product Marketing

Insights, Performance & Learning

Monitor the performance of Store In-App Events and key beats
Support post-launch analysis and documentation of learnings and best practices
Work with Analytics and Growth teams to understand key KPIs and performance drivers
Help turn insights into actionable recommendations for future campaigns




YOUR BACKSTORY

Must-Have

1–2 years of experience in Product Marketing, Marketing, Publishing, or a related role (gaming preferred)
Strong interest in mobile games, live-ops, and player behavior
Basic understanding of app stores and mobile game ecosystems
Comfortable working with multiple stakeholders and deadlines
Highly organized, detail-oriented, and reliable in execution
Strong written communication skills with a player-first mindset
Willingness to learn and grow into a broader Product Marketing role

Nice-to-Have

Experience with mobile F2P games
Basic familiarity with ASO, Store In-App Events, or UA concepts
Experience working with tools like Google Workspace, Excel, Confluence, or Jira
Interest in brand, positioning, and player psychology

What Success Looks Like

Store In-App Events are consistently executed on time, on brief, and with clear performance visibility
Product updates and live-ops beats are clearly and compellingly communicated to players
Product Marketing is seen as a reliable execution partner by Product and Publishing teams
Messaging quality and consistency improve across store and product touchpoints
You build a strong foundation to grow into a full Product Marketing Manager role







Why You'll Love Working With Us

A Global Team, A Shared Purpose

Our strength lies in our diversity. With over 30 nationalities represented across our two locations, we’re united by a shared passion for strategy games and a bold vision for the future. We celebrate what makes each of us unique – our perspectives, our stories, and the paths that led us here.

Work Your Way

Whether you're crafting code from a harbor-view desk in Hamburg or fine-tuning design details under the Maltese sun, we support flexible hybrid work models that empower you to do your best work – wherever you are.

Grow With Us

At Twin Harbour, your journey matters. Our Career Path Framework and mentorship culture provide the clarity and feedback you need to take the next step – whether you’re deepening your expertise or branching into new disciplines. We invest in your growth with language courses, e-learning platforms, and support for conferences and training.

We’re Human First

We believe that great teams are built on trust, respect, and good humor. From game nights to casual drinks, quiz nights, and karaoke, we don’t just work together – we connect, play, and support each other like a true crew.




Join Twin Harbour Interactive

Where Strategy Meets Vision.

Twin Harbour Interactive is a global strategy game powerhouse, shaping the future of long-term multiplayer gaming. With offices in Hamburg and Malta, we design strategy games that bring people together – across borders and generations.

Whether you’re an expert tactician or an imaginative world-builder, there’s a place here for you to grow, thrive, and leave your mark.

Can you bring your marketing experience to our team? Join our team in Berlinand enjoy a collaborative culture that empowers you to build a career you can be proud of.

What you would do as our Marketing Specialist, DAS EMEIA

J oin us to lead impactful international marketing campaigns in Digital Access Solutions (DAS) and grow within a supportive, cross-cultural team.

As an EMEIA Marketing Specialist within the DAS Product Segment Marketing team, you will be responsible for shaping and driving the marketing strategy for specific product lines within the divisional DAS portfolio. Your role will focus on planning, coordinating, and overseeing impactful campaigns that strengthen brand awareness, support commercial objectives, and address diverse target audiences across the EMEIA region.

You will work closely with commercial teams and specialists in product communication, digital marketing, and marketing activation to ensure consistent positioning and alignment across all channels.

You would also:


Develop and lead marketing communications strategy for assigned product lines, including annual planning, budgeting, and content direction.
Create and manage value-driven content and campaigns, such as product launches and industry-specific topics, in collaboration with internal teams and agencies.
Enhance digital presence by optimizing online and social media channels to boost visibility and engagement.
Produce and integrate customer use cases in various formats, ensuring alignment with the central content library.
Collaborate and align with stakeholders, including the Marketing Activation Manager, while using market insights to guide strategic messaging and campaign execution.


The Skills And Experience You Need

We are looking for someone who:


Has at least 10 years in international B2B marketing or within a marketing agency, ideally involving complex products and solutions.
Has a strong track record in lead generation and strategic campaign execution for complex offerings and is interested in the latest MarCom tools.
Is able to turn high-level concepts into actionable plans with precision in execution, budgeting, and process management.
Is proactive, deadline-driven, and capable of managing multiple projects, with strong verbal and written communication skills.
Has degree in Marketing, Communications, or related field; experience in security, building management, or building materials is a plus; has excellent English skills, fluency in additional European languages is advantageous.


What We Offer

We’re passionate about providing amazing opportunities and benefits, so you can continue and progress a lifelong career with us – here’s what we have to offer:


Learning and career development opportunities, whether it’s online learning, management training or enhancing your skills.
A competitive salary and variety of benefits such as performance related bonus etc.


We review applications regularly, so don't wait

We are building diverse, inclusive teams, and encourage applications from everyone who can see themselves working with us. Just set up your profile and apply here.

To make sure your personal data is safe, we don’t look at any applications sent by email or post. If you have any questions about the role or the process, email Stefan Sausenthaler (stefan.sausenthaler @assaabloy.com).

Let’s create a safer and more open world - together!

To find out more about us, visit www.assaabloy.com

We are the ASSA ABLOY Group

Our people have made us the global leader in access solutions. In return, we open doors for them wherever they go. With nearly 63,000 colleagues in more than 70 different countries, we help billions of people experience a more open world. Our innovations make all sorts of spaces – physical and virtual – safer, more secure, and easier to access.

As an employer, we value results – not titles, or backgrounds. We empower our people to build their career around their aspirations and our ambitions – supporting them with regular feedback, training, and development opportunities. Our colleagues think broadly about where they can make the most impact, and we encourage them to grow their role locally, regionally, or even internationally.

As we welcome new people on board, it’s important to us to have diverse, inclusive teams, and we value different perspectives and experiences.

CRM-gestützte Marketingkommunikation & E-Mail-Marketing

Content & Unterstützung digitaler Marketingmaßnahmen


Erstellung und Anpassung von Marketingtexten für Newsletter, Website und Kampagnen

Dieses Team erwartet dich
Werde Teil unseres dynamischen Marketingteams, bestehend aus dreizehn kreativen Köpfen, die leidenschaftlich daran arbeiten, innovative Strategien zu entwickeln und unsere Marke erfolgreich zu positionieren. description_full:
About Agoda

At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world.

Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide.

No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us.

Get to Know our Team:

Agoda's Brand Marketing team is looking for an Analyst to scale our digital marketing & brand research efforts.

As a part of the Brand Media & Measurement team, you will manage digital campaign planning, A/B testing, and reporting across key digital channels like Meta, Twitter, YouTube etc., and also be responsible for management & analysis of brand research projects.

This person will have the opportunity to learn and grow with the global Brand Marketing team based in Thailand. They will play a key role in analyzing all kind of campaign & research data to deliver insights that will shape the team’s strategy and fulfil our ambition of creating an engaging and meaningful brand in the tech-and-travel space.

This role is based out of Agoda’s Global Operations Center in Bangkok, Thailand, giving the successful applicant the once-in-a-lifetime chance to work in one of the world’s most exciting and fast-paced cities, close to some of the most iconic travel destinations, with a fun and talented team. We provide complete relocation for you and your family and excellent benefits.

About The Brand Team

We are the guardians of the Agoda brand, with a mission to make Agoda a trusted name in global travel. Our scope is broad and encompasses various projects and collaborations with other departments at Agoda. In any given week, we could be working on campaign creation and activation (including consumer research, strategy, agency management, media planning, measurement, and testing), producing assets for Agoda’s various marketing channels and our website and app, or designing official Agoda merch for our offices around the globe. We ensure the brand is always looking and sounding its best in everything we do, whether on the world stage or in a company newsletter.

The Brand team is diverse, representing strategists, media planners, project managers, brand managers, creatives, and more, from different walks of life. We have in common a love of travel (and travel deals) and a passion for finding fun and innovative ways to bring the Agoda brand to the world.

In this role, you’ll get to:


Experiment: Conduct A/B testing on platforms like Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, TikTok and YouTube, as well as brand research in-house & with external agencies to demonstrate the impact of campaign activity on brand awareness.
Execute: Own the performance of digital & social campaigns, optimize critical campaign metrics, and keep an eye on data to ensure that media is delivering against the plan.
Learn: Communicate test results & campaign learnings to the team and broader stakeholders through comprehensive analysis, reporting and data visualization. Develop key learnings for different media platforms and markets.
Analyse: Delve into data from digital tests & research studies to gather useful insights for optimization of brand campaigns.
Collaborate: Liaise with external media partners (e.g. Google, Meta) for planning, analysis and execution, and consolidate media data and analysis with digital activity being run in-house.


What you’ll need to succeed:


A minimum of a bachelor’s degree in mathematics, marketing, communication, business, or another related field
At least 2 years of experience in analytically focused positions
Great SQL & Tableau knowledge
Advanced knowledge of Excel, very comfortable using VLOOKUP and Pivot Tables
Great project management, measurement, and planning capability; taking ownership from the start to the finish
Knowledge of statistics and data processing practices (calculating p-values, data cleaning and normalization, regressions, etc.) is a plus

Melde dich.

Unser Ziel ist es, das große Potenzial unserer Mitarbeiter zu nutzen, um neue Lösungen für unsere Kunden zu entwickeln.





Wir suchen dich für den Standort Brüggen als



Category Specialist (m/w/d)



Du möchtest die Lieferperformance eines Unternehmens entscheidend mitgestalten?

In dieser Schlüsselrolle sorgst du dafür, dass Roh- und Verpackungsmaterialien zur richtigen Zeit in der richtigen Menge verfügbar sind - und trägst so maßgeblich zu einer exzellenten OTIF-Performance bei. Gleichzeitig behältst du Kosten und Bestände im Blick, optimierst Lieferantenbeziehungen und treibst Prozessverbesserungen voran. Wenn du Spaß daran hast, gemeinsam mit internen Teams und externen Partnern die Supply Chain jeden Tag ein Stück besser zu machen, bist du hier genau richtig.



DEINE VERANTWORTUNG



Steuerung des gesamten Order-to-Delivery-Prozesses


Pflege von Stammdaten und Bestellparametern


Erstellung von Einkaufsprognosen und Bestellungen


Sicherstellung termingerechter und mengenrichtiger Lieferungen (OTD)


Management von Lieferengpässen, Abweichungen und Reklamationen


Koordination mit Produktion und Planung


Analyse und Vermeidung von Fehl- und Überbeständen


Überwachung kritischer Materialbestände


Unterstützung bei Lieferanten-Onboarding, Lieferantenwechseln und ERP-Tests


Zusammenarbeit mit lokalen Lieferanten inkl. Sicherstellung von Compliance-Anforderungen


Mitarbeit in bereichsübergreifenden Prozessen (z. B. S&OP, Produktlebenszyklus, NQP)





DEIN PROFIL

Wir suchen engagierte Menschen, die unsere Werte Kreativität, Beharrlichkeit und Fairness teilen. Wenn du folgende Qualifikationen mitbringst, bist du in dieser Rolle goldrichtig:






Erfolgreich abgeschlossene kaufmännische Ausbildung oder wirtschaftsnahes Studium - gerne mit Schwerpunkt Einkauf, Supply Chain oder Logistik


Sicherer Umgang mit ERP-Systemen


Verständnis von Bestandsmanagement, Forecasting und Lieferkettenprozessen

Visit us at airplus.com to learn more about a future in corporate payment.

Strategic Account Manager (m/f/d)

Location: Neu-Isenburg | Area: Sales | Contract type: Full time / part time possible

AirPlus is a leading provider of solutions for managing payment for business travel and is seeking for a senior and experienced Strategic Account Manager. The dynamic team which is responsible for the largest customer portfolio of AirPlus. As a Strategic Account Manager, you will be responsible for developing and maintaining long-term relationships with key clients to achieve business objectives.

Your tasks


Revenue Growth & Profitability
Drive top-line revenue growth through strategic account development and value-based selling. Monitor account-level P&L to ensure profitability targets are met or exceeded
Negotiate contract renewals, pricing models, and margin improvements in line with business objectives
Upselling & Cross-Selling Strategy
Identify opportunities to expand product or service adoption within existing accounts
Design and implement structured upselling and cross-selling initiatives based on account maturity and need Negotiate commercial terms to balance customer value with business profitability
Stakeholder Mapping & Influence
Build influence across multiple levels of the client’s organization—procurement, operations, C-suite, etc.
Develop executive-level engagement strategies to position your organization as a strategic partner, not just a vendor
Competitive Positioning & Value Proposition
Position pricing and product strategy in response to competitor moves and market trends
Renewal Strategy & Retention
Deliver Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs) to demonstrate ongoing value and secure long-term commitment

Your qualification


Bachelor’s degree - Sales, Marketing, Finance, Travel or a related field
5+ years of experience in account management, strategic sales, or client relationship roles
Proven track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets, revenue growth, and account profitability
Experience managing enterprise or high-value accounts with complex stakeholder environments
Demonstrated ability to upsell, cross-sell, and expand within existing client accounts
Proficiency in contract negotiation, pricing strategies, and commercial deal structuring
Experience in building and executing strategic account plans aligned to customer and business goals
Ability to build long-term, trust-based relationships with senior stakeholders
Excellent communication, interpersonal, and presentation skills
Strong listening skills with the ability to identify customer pain points and tailor solutions
Experience delivering high-impact QBRs, executive briefings, and strategic reviews
Familiarity with procurement and vendor management processes on the client side
Native German (spoken and written) / Business English (spoken and written)

snacks & drinks
jobticket

am Standort Rheine

Im Bereich D2C (Direct-to-Consumer) suchen wir für unsere starken Marken Landhausküche, Tellerglück und den Bereich Essen auf Rädern einen Marketing Manager D2C (m/w/d). Du verstehst den Marketing-Mix wie kein Zweiter, kannst dich tief in deine Zielgruppe – hochbetagte Menschen sowie Angehörige – hineindenken und setzt starke Online- sowie Offline-Kampagnen in den richtigen Kanälen um? Dann bist du bei uns genau richtig! In unserem Team kombinierst du Strategie mit Kreativität. Mit deiner fachlichen Expertise in crossmedialer Markenkommunikation und Brand Building entlang der Customer Journey bildest du die Schnittstelle zu anderen Fachabteilungen.

Folgende Aufgaben Kommen Auf Dich Zu


Du bist ganzheitlich verantwortlich für unsere zielgruppenspezifischen Marketing-Kampagnen – von der Planung und Umsetzung bis hin zur Auswertung. Darüber hinaus setzt du Kommunikationsprojekte in Zusammenarbeit mit anderen Fachabteilungen aus dem Geschäftsbereich oder externen Partnern um.
Du agierst als Projektleitung bei diversen Marketing-Maßnahmen und verantwortest in Teilen das Marketingbudget.
Im Rahmen der Media- und Kampagnenplanung verantwortest du die datenbasierte Umsetzung des Marketing-Mix, steuerst Maßnahmen wie Newsletter, Offline-Kampagnen, Social Media und Paid Ads und überprüfst regelmäßig deren Performance.
Du führst Marktforschungsmaßnahmen (z. B. Wettbewerbsanalysen, Kundenbefragungen) oder Monitorings zur Identifizierung von Trends, Wettbewerben und Zielgruppenanforderungen durch und wertest sie aus.
Du steuerst externe Agenturen und Freelancer inkl. Briefing, Angebots- und Rechnungsprüfung.


Das Ist Dein Erfolgsrezept

Uns überzeugen authentische Persönlichkeiten, die Verantwortung übernehmen und Weitsicht haben, strukturiert arbeiten und Projekte von der Idee bis zur Umsetzung erfolgreich realisieren können.


Du verfügst über ein abgeschlossenes Hochschulstudium im Bereich BWL o. Ä., idealerweise mit dem Schwerpunkt Marketing, oder eine berufsbegleitende Weiterbildung im Bereich Marketing
Du hast mindestens fünf Jahre Berufserfahrung
Du hast nachweisbare Expertise in der Markenkommunikation (vorzugsweise mit Fokus D2C)
Du denkst analytisch, bist digitalaffin und hast einen sicheren Umgang mit Tools wie CMS-Systemen, Google Analytics sowie Meta
Du bist fachlicher Impulsgeber (m/w/d) mit einem Sinn für Trends und dem Blick für die Brand / Corporate Identity
Du bist proaktiv, kommunikationsstark und mitreißend

Perspektiven:
Als krisensicheres Unternehmen kannst du dich bei apetito auch mit langfristiger Perspektive weiterentwickeln
Finanzielles:
Eine faire Vergütung und ein volles 13. Gehalt (Weihnachtsgeld) sowie Urlaubsgeld in Höhe von 2.500 €
Vermögenswirksame Leistungen, eine geförderte betriebliche Altersvorsorge und das Fahrrad-Leasing gehören bei uns selbstverständlich dazu

30 + 1 Urlaubstage
Flexible Arbeitszeiten – Überstunden kannst du bei uns durch Freizeit wieder ausgleichen
In Rheine erwartet dich unser Betriebsrestaurant mit täglichem Essensgeldzuschuss von 2,65 €

Corporate-Benefits-Programm mit vielen Rabatten namhafter Hersteller (z. B. Elektronik, Urlaubsreisen)
Zahlreiche Angebote zur Vereinbarkeit von Familie und Beruf

Deine Aufgaben bei uns:


Produktziele & Outcomes setzen: Du definierst nutzerorientierte Ziele für unsere interne Developer Plattform und den Cloud-Betrieb.
Reliability, Security & Compliance verantworten: Du stellst sicher, dass die Plattform zuverlässig, sicher und compliant ist – und koordinierst die Anforderungen mit den relevanten Stakeholdern.
Nutzer:innen verstehen: Du erkennst die Probleme und Bedürfnisse interner sowie externer Nutzer:innen und entwickelst im Product Trio passende Lösungen.

Botschafter:in der Produktstrategie: Du schaffst Alignment zwischen Teams, Stakeholdern und Management und machst den Wert unserer Plattform sichtbar.

Du bringst Erfahrung im Produktmanagement sowie in der Zusammenarbeit mit Software-Teams mit.
